query: How many Buddhist texts do you have?
response: The library holds 858 Buddhist documents, with the majority being part of the Pali Canon, which comprises the oldest surviving Buddhist scriptures. Among these texts is the *Diamond Sutra*, known for its teachings on ["transcendent wisdom"](https://siftersearch.com/library/Buddhist/Mahayana%20Sutras/tr-william-gemmell_the-diamond-sutra-vajracchedika-prajnaparamita#p272) that guide one to "The Other Shore." This collection offers a rich tapestry of Buddhist thought and practice.

query: Do you have any Jain texts?
response: Yes, the library includes Jain texts. Specifically, there are [22 documents](https://siftersearch.com/library/Jain/Philosophy/tr-hermann-jacobi_jaina-sutras-part-i-sbe-vol-22#p19) and an additional 5 documents under Jainism. These include the [Akaranga Sutra](https://siftersearch.com/library/Jain/Agamas/tr-hermann-jacobi_akaranga-sutra-book-1#p0), which is part of the Agamas collection, the canonical scriptures of Jainism. This collection reflects the teachings of the Tirthankaras, who are revered as enlightened teachers in the Jain tradition.
